terms.definition: A societal configuration in which public approval is earned by displaying classical virtues such as prudence and duty
A societal configuration in which approval is earned primarily by displaying uniqueness and self-expression
A frequency measure of a word or phrase across a large, dated text corpus (e.g., Google Books)
The reduction of communal identity into isolated individuals
The measurable loss of vocabulary that encodes a cultural norm
The assertion that changes in linguistic frequency predict subsequent behavioral change
A metaphor for the suite of concepts and norms that detect and correct moral deviation in a society
A polity in which default social expectations include honesty and cooperation
A linguistic environment lacking duty-oriented terms
The time at which virtue-term frequency drops below a system-stability threshold, borrowing the symbol from percolation theory
The point at which the rate of change itself changes sign, indicating acceleration or deceleration in a trend
